THIS EF-1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ALONG RIDDLE LANE CAUSING TREE DAMAGE AS WELL AS DAMAGE TO THE OUTFIELD WALL AT THE LORETTO SPORTS COMPLEX. THE TORNADO TRAVELED EAST-NORTHEAST TAKING DOWN ADDITIONAL TREES AND POWERLINES. ON HIGHWAY 43, THE FIRST FARMERS BANK SIGN WAS BLOWN DOWN AS THE TORNADO CONTINUED ACROSS THE HIGHWAY INTO AN OPEN FIELD BEFORE INTENSIFYING CAUSING DAMAGE TO SEVERAL HOMES IN A NEIGHBORHOOD. NUMEROUS SIGNIFICANT TREES WERE SNAPPED/UPROOTED. AT THIS POINT, THE TORNADO WAS AT ITS

*** 1 INJ *** THIS EF-1 TORNADO INITIALLY TOUCHED DOWN ALONG GLENN SPRINGS ROAD DOWNING SEVERAL TREES INCLUDING A LARGE TREE FALLING ON A HOUSE. THE TORNADO THEN CROSSED HIGHWAY 64 CONTINUING ITS DESTRUCTION OF MOSTLY TREES, POWERLINES, AND ROOFS AS IT MOVED THROUGH THE CITY OF LAWRENCEBURG. SEVERAL LARGE TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED RIGHT IN THE HEART OF THE CITY ESPECIALLY TO THE NEIGHBORHOOD SURROUNDING THE PUBLIC LIBRARY. THE TORNADO THEN MOVED EAST-NORTHEAST INTO A MORE RESIDENTIAL NEIGHBORHOOD WHERE
HOUSE DAMAGED AT 4345 GIMLET RD.
REPORTED AT LAWRENCE COUNTY EOC. SUSTAINED WINDS ABOVE 50 MPH AS WELL.

A HOME AND SEVERAL CARPORTS WERE DAMAGED IN THE AREA OF FRONT VIEW ST. AND VALLEY VIEW DRIVE. TIME WAS ESTIMATED BY RADAR.

THIS EF-0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ON OLD UNIONVILLE ROAD BETWEEN THE INTERSECTION OF BEN WILLIAMS ROAD AND HIGHWAY 41 N WHERE SOME LARGE TREE BRANCHES WERE BROKEN OFF OF TREES. ADDITIONAL BRANCHES WERE BROKEN OFF OF TREES ALONG HIGHWAY 41 N AND THE TORNADO CONTINUED TO TRAVEL NORTHEAST WHERE A CARPORT WAS DAMAGED AS WELL AS NUMEROUS TREES SNAPPED NEAR A HOME ALONG GREGORY MILL ROAD. ON OLD NASHVILLE DIRT ROAD NEAR FRANK MARTIN ROAD THERE WERE SEVERAL TREES DOWNED AS WELL AS SOME UTILITY POLES. FURT

A WATER RESCUE WAS CONDUCTED AT KING HOLLOW AND MT. JOY ROADS. NUMEROUS FLOODED ROADS WERE REPORTED ACROSS MAURY COUNTY.

THIS EF-0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ON W GREEN HILL ROAD WHERE IT SNAPPED A FEW TREES. IT CONTINUED EAST-NORTHEAST WHERE TREES SNAPPED ON TOP OF A MOBILE HOME ON PIGEON HILL ROAD. A HOUSE ON SHORT MOUNTAIN ROAD THAT RECEIVED MINOR DAMAGE TO THE FRONT PORCH AND GARAGE. A BARN WAS COMPLETELY DESTROYED ON WOMACK ROAD. NUMEROUS TREES WERE UPROOTED AND SNAPPED ALONG THE PATH BEFORE FINALLY LIFTING NEAR GATH LUCKY ROAD.

THIS EF-1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ON GUM SPRING MOUNTAIN WHERE A HOUSE COMPLETELY LOST ITS ROOF ALONG WITH DOZENS OF TREES GETTING UPROOTED AND SNAPPED. AN OUTBUILDING AND BARN WERE ALSO DAMAGED. THE TORNADO CONTINUED EAST BRINGING DOWN TREES AND POWERLINES BEFORE FINALLY HITTING THE NATIONAL GUARD ARMORY ON MCMINNVILLE HIGHWAY WHERE NUMEROUS LARGE PINE TREES WERE SNAPPED. THE TORNADO LIFTED JUST BEFORE REACHING HIGHWAY 111.
ROADS WERE FLOODED AND SEVERAL WATER RESCUES TOOK PLACE ACROSS MARSHALL COUNTY.
MAIN STREET PARTIALLY BLOCKED DUE TO FLASH FLOOD.

THIS EF-1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST WEST OF BLUE SPRINGS ROAD AND SNAPPED SEVERAL TREES ALONG WITH DESTROYING A BARN. A COVERED PORCH WAS ALSO DESTROYED AT THIS LOCATION AS WELL AS THE ROOF OF A SINGLE STORY HOME. THE TORNADO CONTINUED EASTWARD ACROSS A VALLEY BEFORE CAUSING EXTENSIVE TREE DAMAGE ON JAMES LAMBERT ROAD. SEVERAL OUTBUILDINGS AND BARNS WERE COMPLETELY DESTROYED AS WELL AS EXTENSIVE DAMAGE TO A MOBILE HOME. THE TORNADO WENT UP A MOUNTAIN AND BACK DOWN AND THEN HIT A BARN/OUTBUILDI

THIS EF-0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ON BOX ROAD JUST OFF OF BLOOMING GROVE ROAD WHERE DAMAGE TO SEVERAL FARM BUILDINGS OCCURRED. THE TORNADO CONTINUED NORTHEAST AND DAMAGED MANY LARGE TREES AND TREE BRANCHES BEFORE LIFTING NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF RICHARDSON ROAD AND REVILO ROAD. ADDITIONAL DAMAGE WAS REPORTED ON A FARM NEAR THAT INTERSECTION THAT INCLUDED BARN AND ROOF DAMAGE BEFORE THE TORNADO LIFTED.

WIDESPREAD STRAIGHT-LINE WIND DAMAGE WAS OBSERVED ACROSS THE SOUTHERN HALF OF LAWRENCE AND GILES COUNTIES IN ADDITION TO THE EF-0 TORNADO IN SOUTHEAST LAWRENCE COUNTY. NUMEROUS TREES WERE UPROOTED OR SNAPPED AS WELL AS SEVERAL OUTBUILDINGS AND BARNS DAMAGED ACROSS THIS AREA DUE TO A VERY STRONG REAR-FLANK DOWNDRAFT. THE STRONGEST WINDS AND MOST SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE WERE OBSERVED ON GUNTER RIDGE ROAD IN GILES COUNTY WHERE DOZENS OF LARGE HARDWOODS WERE UPROOTED AND SNAPPED.

NUMEROUS TREES...POWER LINES...POWER POLES...AND RAILROAD CROSSING BARS WERE BLOWN DOWN ACROSS TULLAHOMA WITH MANY ROADS BLOCKED. THE METAL ROOF OF A RESTAURANT ON EAST LINCOLN STREET WAS BLOWN OFF AND A FALLEN TREE HEAVILY DAMAGED A MOBILE HOM ON EAST GRIZZARD STREET.

AN EF-2 TORNADO STARTED IN BENTON COUNTY NW OF CAMDEN THEN MOVED ACROSS THE TENNESSEE RIVER INTO HUMPHREYS COUNTY AROUND 1120 PM CST CAUSING EF-1 DAMAGE TO HUNDREDS OF TREES ON CRYSTAL SPRINGS ROAD. SEVERAL DOZEN TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN ALONG LUCAS RIDGE ROAD...HEMBY BRANCH ROAD...CLYDETON ROAD...BRAZZLE ROAD AND STEPHENSON ROAD NEAR HIGHWAY 13 BEFORE THE TORNADO LIFTED. TOTAL PATH LENGTH ACROSS BENTON AND HUMPHREYS COUNTIES OF 18.95 MILES AND PATH WIDTH OF 250 YARDS.

THIS EF-0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ALONG CURTIS CHAPEL ROAD JUST NORTHWEST OF MCEWEN WHERE IT DAMAGED A SMALL BARN. IT CONTINUED EASTWARD AND UPROOTED SOME TREES ALONG CCC ROAD AS WELL AS CAUSED SOME MINOR ROOF DAMAGE TO A HOME. ADDITIONAL DAMAGE WAS FOUND ON TUMMINS ROAD WHERE A FEW TREES WERE UPROOTED BEFORE THE TORNADO LIFTED. PATH LENGTH 4.8 MILES AND PATH WIDTH 50 YARDS.
HAIL UP TO GOLF BALL SIZE REPORTED SOUTH OF VANLEER
NUMEROUS TWITTER REPORTS AND PHOTOS SHOWED HAIL AS LARGE AS 3 INCHES IN DIAMETER FELL IN AND AROUND THE CHARLOTTE AREA. NUMEROUS CARS SUFFERED BROKEN WINDSHIELDS AND SOME HOMES HAD ROOF DAMAGE.
PICTURE OF A COUPLE HAILSTONES AT LEAST THE SIZE OF BASEBALLS FROM DICKSON RECEIVED ON TSPOTTER.
TSPOTTER REPORT OF HAIL LARGER THAN QUARTERS FALLING IN VANLEER.

*** 5 FATAL, 220 INJ *** A STRONG LONG-TRACK EF-3 TORNADO BEGAN IN FAR WESTERN DAVIDSON COUNTY AND TRACKED THROUGH NASHVILLE PASSING JUST NORTH OF DOWNTOWN BEFORE CAUSING 2 DEATHS IN EAST NASHVILLE. THE TORNADO CONTINUED INTO WILSON COUNTY WITH A 6-MILE SWATH OF EF-3 DAMAGE IN MT. JULIET WHERE 3 MORE DEATHS OCCURRED. THE TORNADO CONTINUED ALONG AND PARALLEL TO I-40 THROUGH LEBANON THEN WEAKENED AS IT MOVED INTO SMITH COUNTY BEFORE FINALLY LIFTING SE OF GORDONSVILLE. PATH LENGTH 60.13 MILES AND PATH WIDTH 800 YARDS.

AN EF-0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR CLUB SPRINGS ROAD JUST NORTH OF THE CANEY FORK RIVER AND MOVED ENE ACROSS VERY HILLY TERRAIN TAKING DOWN SEVERAL TREES. SEVERAL HOMES WERE DAMAGED IN WESTERN PUTNAM COUNTY AS WELL AS SEVERAL OUTBUILDINGS ON ST. MARYS ROAD. THE TORNADO CONTINUED TO ROCK SPRINGS ROAD WITH MINOR DAMAGE OCCURRING TO OUTBUILDINGS AND TREES BEFORE THE TORNADO LIFTED JUST WEST OF STANTON ROAD. PATH LENGTH 5.99 MILES AND PATH WIDTH 50 YARDS.
SEVERAL BUILDINGS SUFFERED EXTENSIVE DAMAGE AROUND FIVE POINTS IN EAST NASHVILLE.
*** 2 FATAL *** CONFIRMED TORNADO IN EAST NASVHILLE.

*** 18 FATAL, 88 INJ *** A VIOLENT EF-4 TORNADO TRACKED ACROSS CENTRAL PUTMAN COUNTY FROM BAXTER TO COOKEVILLE DESTROYING DOZENS OF HOMES AND CAUSING 88 INJURIES AND 18 FATALITIES. THE WORST DAMAGE WAS ON AND AROUND HENSLEY DRIVE WHERE MANY HOMES WERE COMPLETELY DESTROYED. THE TORNADO RAPIDLY DISSIPATED ON N FRANKLIN AVENUE JUST WEST OF COOKEVILLE REGIONAL MEDICAL CENTER. PATH LENGTH 8.23 MILES AND PATH WIDTH 500 YARDS.
SEVERAL HOMES DESTROYED WITH ROOFS REMOVED AND MANY WALLS COLLAPSED NEAR CATALPA DRIVE IN MOUNT JULIET.

A VERY BRIEF AND WEAK EF-0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST OFF OF HIGHWAY 70 AND OVERSTREET DRIVE SOUTHEAST OF COOKEVILLE IN THE DRY VALLEY AND GOFFTON AREA. DAMAGE WAS MOSTLY TO THE ROOF OF ONE HOUSE, METAL GARAGE, AND AN OUTBUILDING. THERE WERE ALSO SEVERAL TREES WITH LARGE BRANCHES BROKEN OFF. PATH LENGTH 0.23 MILES AND PATH WIDTH 25 YARDS.
A 60 MPH WIND GUST RECORDED AT THE NWS OFFICE.
DIME TO HALF DOLLAR SIZE HAIL. DELAYED REPORT.

AN EF-0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR HIGHWAY 127 AND ATKINS ROAD WHERE TREES WERE UPROOTED AND ONE SINGLE WIDE MOBILE HOME SUSTAINED ROOF DAMAGE. THE TORNADO MOVED NE AND CROSSED SMITH ROAD DESTROYING TWO OUTBUILDINGS AND DAMAGING TWO HOMES. MORE OUTBUILDINGS WERE DESTROYED ON BEATY ROAD AND TREES WERE UPROOTED ON COOL SPRINGS ROAD. THE TORNADO CONTINUED EASTWARD INTO THE CATOOSA WILDLIFE MANAGEMENT AREA AND POSSIBLY INTO MORGAN COUNTY BUT THE EXACT PATH IS UNCERTAIN PENDING MORE DATA.

A DAMAGE SURVEY BY WARREN COUNTY EMA ALONG WITH VIDEO BY NEARBY RESIDENTS INDICATE A VERY BRIEF WEAK EF-0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN EXTREME SOUTHWEST WARREN COUNTY BETWEEN MORRISON AND VIOLA ALONG CECIL LAXSON ROAD. ONE BARN SUSTAINED SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE AND SEVERAL TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN ALONG THE ROADWAY AND IN ADJACENT ORCHARDS. PATH LENGTH 1/4 MI AND PATH WIDTH 25 YDS.

UPDATED INFO...A DAMAGE SURVEY BY WARREN COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT ALONG WITH VIDEO BY NEARBY RESIDENTS INDICATE A BRIEF WEAK EF-0 TORNADO INTERMITTENTLY TOUCHED DOWN IN SOUTHWEST WARREN COUNTY BETWEEN MORRISON AND VIOLA. ONE BARN SUSTAINED SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE ON CECIL LAXSON RD AND SEVERAL TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN ALONG THE ROADWAY AND IN ADJACENT ORCHARDS. ANOTHER SMALL BARN WAS DESTROYED ON FRED HOOVER RD WITH A DAMAGE PATH NOTED THROUGH CORN FIELDS. PATH LENGTH 1.6 MI AND PATH WIDTH 25 YDS.

A DAMAGE SURVEY BY PUTNAM COUNTY EMA ALONG WITH RADAR DATA INDICATE A BRIEF EF-0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN SOUTH OF BAXTER. ONE HOME ON PALMER LN SUFFERED GARAGE DOOR DAMAGE NEAR THE START OF THE PATH AND A MOBILE HOME LOST PART OF ITS ROOF ON THOMAS TWIN OAK RD AT PALMER LN. A BARN WAS DAMAGED ON MOSS RD NORTH OF ALTON BOYD RD. SEVERAL TREES WERE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED ALONG THE INTERMITTENT DAMAGE PATH. PATH LENGTH 2.83 MI AND PATH WIDTH 50 YDS.

DRONE FOOTAGE FROM NASHSEVEREWX SHOWED THE TORNADO CONTINUED TO NEAR I-840 AND BENDING CHESTNUT ROAD BEFORE BECOMING OUTFLOW DOMINANT STRAIGHT LINE WINDS.
